title: Container in pod fails to start under Kata
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Starting a pod using Kata fails with `Error: open /run/vc/sbs/<pod-name>: no such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error`:

```bash
$ sudo podman --runtime=kata run -d --name=test-container --pod=new:test-pod registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora sleep 60; sudo podman ps -a
Error: open /run/vc/sbs/test-pod: no such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                 COMMAND  CREATED       STATUS   PORTS   NAMES
8c14790450d2  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2           1 second ago  Created          b3efaa976a24-infra
```

This applies whether creating the pod and container with separate commands or a single command.

The same commands work fine under the default runtime (runc):

```bash
$ sudo podman run -d --name=test-container --pod=new:test-pod registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora sleep 60; sudo podman ps -a
1767e6da354465563e0f727a36125699c3a057d4bd0daf905b35ef614933f9a0
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                     COMMAND  CREATED                 STATUS                     PORTS   NAMES
1767e6da3544  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest  sleep 60  Less than a second ago  Up Less than a second ago          test-container
aa1e992155b5  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                               1 second ago            Created                            a9c9b54575e6-infra
```

The container successfully starts in a pod when using `podman play kube`:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: test-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: test-container
    image: registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora
    command:
    - sleep
    - 60
```

```bash
$ sudo podman --runtime=kata play kube test-pod.yaml; sudo podman ps -a
Pod:
d913c3346db7b4abe15539f1dd7bc28112678ab92a217d4ba318050aec4c1b53
Container:
c1c3e30f4c58e594144ecbf2cbce41f6efe32f5143dabba3d84c91872137f056

CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                              COMMAND   CREATED        STATUS                     PORTS   NAMES
1fe3944509d6  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                         2 seconds ago  Up Less than a second ago          d913c3346db7-infra
c1c3e30f4c58  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  sleep 60  2 seconds ago  Up Less than a second ago          test-pod-test-container
```


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.4-1.fc32.x86_64
```

comments.body:
Log messages from `kata-runtime` in journald after running `sudo podman --runtime=kata run -d --name=test-container --pod=new:test-pod registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora sleep 60`:

```
kata-runtime[43069]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.498699589+01:00" level=info msg="loaded configuration" arch=amd64 command=create file=/usr/share/kata-containers/defaults/configuration.toml format=TOML name=kata-runtime pid=43069 source=katautils
kata-runtime[43069]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.499061087+01:00" level=info msg="vsock supported" arch=amd64 command=create name=kata-runtime pid=43069 source=katautils
kata-runtime[43069]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.499121891+01:00" level=info msg="VSOCK supported, configure to not use proxy" arch=amd64 command=create name=kata-runtime pid=43069 source=katautils
kata-runtime[43069]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.499150933+01:00" level=info arch=amd64 arguments="\"create --bundle /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/b4c15b704b95720bd7e6c9fcd7bd178e0ce13652b86e0a758047fd730686933d/userdata --pid-file /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/b4c15b704b95720bd7e6c9fcd7bd178e0ce13652b86e0a758047fd730686933d/userdata/pidfile b4c15b704b95720bd7e6c9fcd7bd178e0ce13652b86e0a758047fd730686933d\"" command=create commit= name=kata-runtime pid=43069 source=runtime version=1.11.0
kata-runtime[43069]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.500103384+01:00" level=error msg="open /run/vc/sbs/test-pod: no such file or directory" arch=amd64 command=create container=b4c15b704b95720bd7e6c9fcd7bd178e0ce13652b86e0a758047fd730686933d name=kata-runtime pid=43069 source=runtime
kata-runtime[43081]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.534529805+01:00" level=info msg="loaded configuration" arch=amd64 command=delete file=/usr/share/kata-containers/defaults/configuration.toml format=TOML name=kata-runtime pid=43081 source=katautils
kata-runtime[43081]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.534716636+01:00" level=info msg="vsock supported" arch=amd64 command=delete name=kata-runtime pid=43081 source=katautils
kata-runtime[43081]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.534754422+01:00" level=info msg="VSOCK supported, configure to not use proxy" arch=amd64 command=delete name=kata-runtime pid=43081 source=katautils
kata-runtime[43081]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.534779767+01:00" level=info arch=amd64 arguments="\"delete --force b4c15b704b95720bd7e6c9fcd7bd178e0ce13652b86e0a758047fd730686933d\"" command=delete commit= name=kata-runtime pid=43081 source=runtime version=1.11.0
kata-runtime[43081]: time="2020-08-16T17:14:53.534861614+01:00" level=warning msg="Failed to get container, force will not fail: Container ID (b4c15b704b95720bd7e6c9fcd7bd178e0ce13652b86e0a758047fd730686933d) does not exist" arch=amd64 command=delete container=b4c15b704b95720bd7e6c9fcd7bd178e0ce13652b86e0a758047fd730686933d name=kata-runtime pid=43081 source=runtime
```
Not sure how this is a podman issue versus a Kata issue.  Kata is reporting an error, and I know others have gotten kata to run with podman.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I never heard back an explanation why this was a podman issue, closing.
